page: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It features an Intel Xeon 2286G processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 12 physical cores and 12 dedicated vCPUs at a base clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server is equipped with a 1920 GB SSD, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance characteristics. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, falls within the average range, with scores of 2012.7 and 15194.54 respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression performs exceptionally well, reaching top-tier speeds of 35.51 GB/sec, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows strong performance at 13.91 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively weak, recording 32.51 GB/sec for read operations and 14.71 GB/sec for write operations. PassMark testing yields a top-tier memory score of 3453.69, alongside average scores for database operations and CPU extended instructions.

This server offers a cost-effective entry point into dedicated bare metal resources, providing high-frequency physical cores without virtualization overhead. Given the 1920 GB local SSD storage and average database operation benchmarks, it is well-suited for localized database hosting, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks. It can also handle lightweight machine learning tasks, though performance drops significantly on larger models, as evidenced by weak inference speeds on 7B parameter models.
description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a dedicated bare metal server from the Bare Metal SSD family, powered by an Intel Xeon 2286G x86_64 processor. It features 12 physical cores and 12 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 1920 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show strong single-threaded compression performance, with gzip compression reaching top-tier speeds of 35.51 GB/sec, and an average PassMark database score of 6000.57. While large-block memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, the server's dedicated hardware profile makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance without virtualization overhead. It is a practical choice for static web serving, database hosting, and general-purpose utility workloads that benefit from fast local storage and high-frequency dedicated cores.
og_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a bare metal server featuring a 12-core Intel Xeon 2286G CPU, 32 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB SSD, delivering dedicated performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a dedicated bare metal server with a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 32 GB RAM, and 1920 GB SSD storage for consistent workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-frequency physical cores with fast local SSD storage for consistent, non-virtualized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 2286G processor with 12 dedicated physical cores at 4.0 GHz.
1920 GB of local SSD storage for fast data access.
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier PassMark memory benchmark results.
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance of 35.51 GB/sec.
Optimized for dedicated database hosting, static web serving, and utility workloads.
